yogurt-and-orange-cake-a-recipe-comedera.name: Yogurt And Orange Cake: A Recipe
yogurt-and-orange-cake-a-recipe-comedera.original_name: Bizcocho de yogur y naranja: una receta clásica

ingredients.item: 1  yogur natural (usaremos el vaso como medida)

instructions: It purifies the oven at 180-3-with heat up and down.
Lava bien la naranja y ralla la parte externa, evitando la parte blanca. Exprime la naranja y reserva el zumo.
In a large bowl, beat the eggs with the sugar until the mixture is sparkling and has increased its volume considerably.
Incorpora el yogur y el aceite a la mezcla de huevos, batiendo a velocidad media hasta integrar completamente.
Toss the flour with the yeast and salt. It gradually adds this mixture to the moist ingredients and mixes with wrapped movements.
Add the fringes and orange juice and gently integrate up to a homogenous mass.
Prepare the mold with butter or oil and sprinkle with flour and remove the excess.
Vierte la masa en el molde y golpea suavemente contra la superficie para eliminar posibles burbujas de aire.
Hornea durante 45-50 minutos, o hasta que al insertar un palillo en el centro salga limpio.
Let rest in the mold for 10 minutes before breaking. Fold completely onto a grid.

young-beans-sauteed-with-garlic-pequerec.name: Young Beans Sautéed with Garlic

ingredients.item: 2 tazas de habas tiernas, limpias y peladas
Optional: lemon juice to give a citrus touch

instructions: Wash and put them up. You can remove tipples and remove skin from outside to get more tender and soft beans.

If you wish to remove your skin, scale up the tender beans for 2 minutes in boiling water. It goes into an ice bowl to cool them and keep their green color intense. Once the beans are cold, gently tighten a corner of the bean between your fingers. The skin outside should slide easily, revealing the tender bean inside.
In a large frying pan, heat olive oil with medium heat. Add minced garlic teeth and scattering till fringes but avoid them from getting too golden.
Add the tender beans to the frying pan and leap for about 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally. We want them to be tender but they still have a light crunch.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Can squeeze some lemon juice to enhance flavors.
Remove from the fire when the beans are tender but with an still vibrant color. It serves hot as an accompaniment or even as a light main plate.
